local_names.romanized_local: Jī ròu tí kǎ tǎ kě bǐng

aliases: Jī ròu tí kǎ tǎ kě bǐng

romanized: Jī ròu tí kǎ tǎ kě bǐng

region_of_origin: Singapore (fusion dish served at Indian-Mexican restaurants)
food_subgroup: Poultry-based fusion dish; marinated grilled chicken in soft tortillas

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic taco class (FOODON_00002764). No specific class exists for chicken tikka tacos or Indian-Mexican fusion tacos. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers taco as a generic category. No specific code exists for chicken tikka tacos. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for chicken tikka tacos or fusion taco dishes. None assigned.

main_ingredients: tikka spice blend (cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli, garam masala)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 soft-shell chicken tikka tacos, each approximately 110 g, totalling approximately 330 g, as sold at Indian-Mexican fusion restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken tikka (grilled, spiced) and soft flour tortilla — component-based estimation using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, cooked, roasted (NDB 05013) and Tortillas, ready-to-bake or fry, flour (NDB 18364), combined with standard tikka marinade and condiment additions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for chicken tikka tacos exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 45% grilled spiced chicken (tikka), 35% soft flour tortilla, 10% condiments and sauce, 10% fresh garnish (onion, coriander, lime). Chicken tikka per-100g values derived from USDA SR Legacy grilled chicken breast with spice adjustment; tortilla values from USDA SR Legacy flour tortilla. Composite weighted average calculated per 100 g of assembled taco. Values are estimates and carry medium confidence.

gi_source.title: Soft flour tortilla and mixed chicken-grain dish GI estimation — analogous to 'wheat flour tortilla wrap with mixed filling' and 'chapati' GI references from the University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken tikka tacos. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle being soft wheat flour tortilla (GI ~52 per University of Sydney GI Database for wheat flour flatbread/tortilla analogues) and the moderating effect of protein-rich chicken tikka filling and spices. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 20.5 × 3.30) / 100 = 52 × 67.65 / 100 ≈ 35.2. Value documented as 35.2 in field.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 420 mg per 100 g; a 3-piece serving (330 g) delivers approximately 1386 mg sodium, representing 60% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 tacos per meal and avoid additional high-sodium condiments such as soy sauce or extra BBQ sauce to keep sodium intake within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) and high carbohydrate load per 3-piece serving (~67.7 g carbohydrates); the protein content from chicken tikka (~46.9 g per serving) partially attenuates postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume no more than 1–2 tacos per sitting, pair with non-starchy vegetables, and monitor postprandial blood glucose, as the total carbohydrate load from flour tortillas may cause a moderate glycaemic rise despite the low-to-medium GI.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 1.9 g per 100 g (6.3 g per serving) and sodium approximately 1386 mg per serving; grilled preparation method is preferable to fried alternatives and limits additional fat intake.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose grilled over fried chicken tikka preparations, limit serving size to 2 tacos, and avoid high-fat sauces to keep saturated fat and sodium within cardioprotective dietary targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Tikka Tacos (3 Pieces) is an Indian-Mexican fusion dish served at specialty restaurants in Singapore, notably at establishments such as Fiesta Mexico – Three Amigos and Three Amigos Mexican and Indian at Boat Quay. The dish consists of three soft wheat flour tortillas each filled with grilled chicken tikka — boneless chicken pieces marinated in spiced yoghurt containing cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli powder, and garam masala — then grilled or tandoor-cooked until charred and juicy. The filling is typically garnished with fresh coriander, sliced onion, lime wedges, and a smoky BBQ or mint-coriander chutney. The flavour profile combines the smoky, spiced warmth of Indian tikka with the handheld convenience of a Mexican taco format. Nutritionally, each 3-piece serving (approximately 330 g) provides roughly 653 kcal, 46.9 g protein, 22.4 g fat, and 67.7 g carbohydrates. The protein density from the chicken tikka filling makes this a relatively satiating meal. Sodium content is moderately high at approximately 1386 mg per serving, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low-to-medium), driven primarily by the soft wheat flour tortilla, with the protein and spice components moderating the glycaemic response. This dish is suitable for halal diets and contains gluten (wheat tortilla) and dairy (yoghurt marinade). It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy intolerances. The fusion format reflects Singapore's multicultural food culture, where Indian and Mexican culinary traditions are creatively combined.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 3 chicken tikka tacos?
Is chicken tikka taco good for diabetes?
Three Amigos chicken tikka taco calories
Soft shell chicken tikka taco GI

confidence_reason: No direct entry for chicken tikka tacos exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, USDA FoodData Central). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA SR Legacy data for grilled chicken breast and soft flour tortilla as the primary components. GI is an analogous estimate based on soft wheat flour tortilla GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the estimation methodology and absence of a direct source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-based estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for grilled chicken breast (NDB 05013) and soft flour tortilla (NDB 18364), weighted at approximately 45% chicken tikka, 35% tortilla, 10% sauce/condiments, 10% garnish. These are estimates and should be replaced with laboratory analysis or a direct approved-source entry if available. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. 3. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on soft wheat flour tortilla GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for chicken tikka tacos was found in approved sources. Glycemic load per 3-piece serving (330 g) = 52 × 67.65 / 100 = 35.2. The gi_source notes field documents the full GL calculation. 4. The cuisine is classified as Fusion (Indian-Mexican) rather than purely Mexican, reflecting the actual dish composition. The input record listed cuisine as Mexican, which is partially correct given the taco format, but the dominant flavour profile and preparation are Indian (tikka). 5. Halal status is listed as true based on the restaurant context (Indian-Muslim and Mexican fusion restaurants in Singapore commonly serve halal chicken); this should be verified against restaurant-specific halal certification. 6. Price SGD 25.00 for 3 pieces is preserved from the input record as contextual metadata but is not included in the nutrient record per Veritas scope. 7. Serving size of 330 g (3 × ~110 g tacos) is an estimate based on typical soft taco serving weights; actual restaurant portion may vary.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that glycemic_load_per_serving was incorrectly set to 22.1 in first pass (used per-100g carbs instead of per-serving carbs). Corrected to 35.2 in iteration 2.

Iteration 2: Validator feedback indicated Q8 failure due to 'aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases'. Upon review, identified a typo in the Hindi alias 'चिकन टिक्ka टैकोस' in the flat 'aliases' list (corrected to 'चिकन टिक्का टैकोस') and the 'romanized_local' alias 'Jī ròu tí kǎ tǎ kě bǐng' was missing from the flat 'aliases' list (added). These corrections ensure perfect consistency between 'aliases_by_language' and 'aliases'. Verified all hard gates pass. Score confirmed 8/8. Final decision set to PASS.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
